items.name: Chiron Real Estate Inc.
items.headline: Chiron sells 7 rehab facilities for $217M, retains 15% JV stake
whatChanged: Sold portfolio of 7 inpatient rehabilitation facilities to a joint venture for $217.0M aggregate sale price, closed June 29, 2026
Acquired 15% ownership interest in the acquiring JV; Properties deconsolidated and will be accounted for as an unconsolidated joint venture investment
Estimated gain on sale of $70.7M; net cash proceeds retained of $194.9M after closing/transaction costs and $16.3M JV investment
Pro forma FY2025 revenue declines from $148.2M to $131.6M (removing ~$16.6M of property revenue), but pro forma net income swings to $53.7M from a $(6.9M) loss due to the gain
Pro forma Q1 2026 revenue drops from $38.1M to $33.9M; pro forma net loss widens to $(1.0M) from $1.7M as quarterly operating income from the properties is removed
items.decisionTakeaway: Chiron monetized seven rehab facilities at a $70.7M estimated gain while retaining 15% upside via the JV, injecting ~$194.9M of cash but reducing annualized revenue by roughly $16.6M going forward.

keyMetrics.netCashProceedsRetained: $194.9M (after $2.8M closing costs, $2.9M transaction costs, $16.3M JV investment)

items.name: Forgent Power Solutions, Inc.
items.headline: FPS prices $2.14B secondary offering at $49/share
whatChanged: Forgent Power Solutions announced a 43,650,000-share secondary offering at $49.00/share for total gross proceeds of ~$2.14B, priced July 1, 2026

Offering split: 14,555,925 primary shares (~$695M to company) used to purchase and redeem Opco LLC Interests from Forgent Parent II/III LP, and 29,094,075 secondary shares (~$1.39B to selling stockholders Forgent Parent I/IV LP)
Underwriters hold overallotment option for up to 6,547,500 additional shares (2,183,410 from company, 4,364,090 from selling stockholders)
Upon completion, FPS ceases to be a 'controlled company' under NYSE rules as Neos Partners' economic/voting stake declines
Revenue grew 86% YoY to $958.4M for the nine months ended March 31, 2026; Backlog rose ~20% to ~$2.4B from March 31 to May 31, 2026
items.decisionTakeaway: A large ~$2.14B secondary at $49 (vs $49.90 last close) simplifies FPS's Up-C structure via Opco unit redemptions while allowing sponsor Neos to monetize ~$1.39B, with the company losing controlled-company status post-close.

items.name: Core & Main, Inc.
items.headline: CNM adds $800M Tranche F term loans to refinance Tranche D
whatChanged: Sixth Amendment to Term Loan Credit Agreement dated July 1, 2026 establishes $800M in new Tranche F Term Loan commitments
Proceeds of Tranche F loans plus high yield debt offering to repay all outstanding Tranche D Term Loans and fund general corporate purposes
Tranche F priced at ABR +0.75% / Term SOFR +1.75%, a 25bp reduction vs Tranche D's 1.00%/2.00% margins
JPMorgan Chase Bank serves as Administrative Agent; amendment executed by CFO Robyn Bradbury
Companion $750M Senior Notes due 2034 offering was announced in prior 8-K dated June 25, 2026
items.decisionTakeaway: Core & Main is refinancing its Tranche D term loans with $800M of new Tranche F term loans at 25bp lower margins alongside a $750M senior notes offering, reducing interest costs without increasing total debt meaningfully.

keyMetrics.Use of Proceeds: Repay all Tranche D Term Loans + fees + general corporate purposes

items.name: SoundHound AI, Inc.
items.headline: S-4/A: Houlihan Lokey consents to inclusion of fairness opinion in SoundHound-LivePerson merger filing
whatChanged: Amendment No. 1 to SoundHound's S-4 registration statement includes Houlihan Lokey's fairness opinion as financial advisor to LivePerson
Houlihan Lokey consents to references to its April 21, 2026 opinion across multiple sections of the joint proxy statement/prospectus
The opinion is included as Annex B and referenced in sections covering the mergers, board recommendation, and background of the mergers
items.decisionTakeaway: The filing confirms the SoundHound-LivePerson merger is advancing through regulatory review with the financial advisor's fairness opinion formally incorporated into the amended registration statement.

items.name: SoundHound AI, Inc.
items.headline: SoundHound amends LivePerson merger agreement with two-step structure
whatChanged: SoundHound AI filed an amended and restated merger agreement (dated July 2, 2026) to acquire LivePerson, Inc., replacing the original agreement from April 21, 2026

The amended deal adds a second merger step: Merger Sub I merges into LivePerson (stock consideration), then Merger Sub II merges into the surviving entity to convert TASE Shares for cash (Per Share Cash Merger Consideration), creating a two-tier structure for Israeli-exchange shares vs. regular shares
Company Warrants outstanding immediately prior to the First Effective Time will be cancelled without any payment
The agreement references concurrent Notes Restructuring Transactions under which holders of Secured Notes agreed to release and deem satisfied the Secured Notes
LivePerson had 12,135,767 shares of common stock issued and outstanding as of April 17, 2026, plus 1,031,021 shares issuable upon exercise of Company Warrants
items.decisionTakeaway: The amended two-step merger structure addresses TASE-shareholder treatment with a separate cash-out merger, indicating the parties are resolving structural complexities to advance the SoundHound-LivePerson deal toward closing.

items.name: Procore Technologies, Inc.
items.headline: Director Erin Chapple resigns; board shrinks to 10
whatChanged: Erin Chapple voluntarily resigned from Procore's Board effective June 30, 2026, citing no disagreement with the company
Board approved a reduction in board size from 11 to 10 directors, effective immediately upon Chapple's resignation
items.decisionTakeaway: A routine single-director departure with no stated disagreement and a proportional board-size reduction has no bearing on Procore's operations or outlook.

items.name: SoundHound AI, Inc.
items.headline: SoundHound amends LivePerson merger agreement with two-step structure
whatChanged: SoundHound AI filed an amended and restated merger agreement (dated July 2, 2026) replacing the original April 21, 2026 agreement to acquire LivePerson, Inc.

The amended deal restructures into a two-step merger: Merger Sub I merges into LivePerson (First Merger), then Merger Sub II merges into the surviving entity (Second Merger), with an intermediate holding company structure added

Company Common Stock (non-TASE) converts to Per Share Merger Consideration (Parent stock) at the First Effective Time; TASE Shares convert to Per Share Cash Merger Consideration at the Second Effective Time
Company Warrants outstanding at the First Effective Time will be cancelled without any payment
Concurrent Notes Restructuring Agreement releases/deems satisfied the Secured Notes held by noteholders in connection with the transaction
items.decisionTakeaway: The amended merger agreement adds a two-step structure with cash treatment for TASE-listed shares and warrant cancellation, refining the original April deal terms for SoundHound's acquisition of LivePerson.

items.name: Standex International Corporation
items.headline: Standex to acquire 9.9% of Narayan Powertech for $64M

Governing law is India with SIAC arbitration in Mumbai for dispute resolution
items.decisionTakeaway: Standex is spending approximately $64M to increase its ownership in Indian entity Narayan Powertech to near-full consolidation, a bolt-on that is modest relative to SXI's $4B market cap but signals continued M&A execution in its Electronics/engineering platform.

items.name: ADC Therapeutics SA

items.decisionTakeaway: Retention awards totaling ~$2.9M in cash plus ~1.1M RSUs for three NEOs signal the board is locking in key leadership through mid-2027 following a strategic reorganization and ahead of critical LOTIS-5 regulatory milestones.

keyMetrics.RSU vesting date: earlier of June 30, 2027 or qualifying termination

items.name: Circle Internet Group

items.name: AXT, Inc.

items.name: Iovance Biotherapeutics, Inc.
items.headline: Iovance reaffirms FY26 guidance, updates pipeline data across solid tumors
whatChanged: Q2 2026 revenue guidance of $86M–$88M and FY 2026 revenue guidance of $350M–$370M reiterated in corporate overview presentation
Cash position of ~$319M as of 3/31/26 with runway into 2028; 1Q26 gross margin was 41% impacted by non-recurring one-time costs
Real-world melanoma data showed 44% ORR overall (52% in ≤2 prior lines) from 41 evaluable patients; 5-year pooled analysis showed 31.4% ORR, 19.7% OS, mDOR 36.5 months
NSCLC cohort (IOV-LUN-202) interim data: 25.6% ORR with mDOR not reached at 25.4 months median follow-up; Fast Track Designation confirmed

Pipeline expansion: sarcoma Phase 2 registrational trial to commence 2Q26 (50% ORR in initial data); endometrial cancer 40% confirmed ORR / 100% DCR; next-gen IOV-5001 IND-cleared for Phase 1/2 in 2H26
items.decisionTakeaway: Iovance's corporate overview reaffirms FY26 revenue guidance of $350M–$370M while broadening the clinical dataset across NSCLC, sarcoma, and endometrial cancer, supporting the pipeline expansion thesis beyond melanoma.

keyMetrics.NSCLC ORR (IOV-LUN-202 interim): 25.6% (n=39); mDOR not reached at 25.4 mo follow-up

whatChanged: Tesla filed an 8-K under Item 2.02 announcing a July 2, 2026 press release containing its Q2 2026 financial results
The filing furnishes Exhibit 99.1 (the Q2 2026 update press release) but the 8-K body itself contains no financial figures or operational details
This follows the Q1 2026 update (filed April 22) which reported $22.4B revenue, $0.9B GAAP operating income, and $0.5B GAAP net income
No guidance changes, M&A, capital actions, or management changes are disclosed in the 8-K cover filing itself
items.decisionTakeaway: Tesla furnished its Q2 2026 earnings press release via 8-K, and investors must look to Exhibit 99.1 for the actual financial results and any changes to the outlook previously issued in Q1.

items.name: Columbia Financial, Inc.
items.headline: Columbia Financial clears MHC conversion and Northfield merger approvals
whatChanged: Stockholders approved the Plan of Conversion and Reorganization at the June 25, 2026 annual meeting, converting Columbia Bank MHC from mutual to fully public stock holding company form
Depositors approved the Conversion at a Special Meeting of Members of the MHC held June 29, 2026
Resolicitation of maximum purchasers in the subscription offering concluded June 30, 2026
Acquisition of Northfield Bancorp will close simultaneously upon completion of the Conversion
items.decisionTakeaway: With both stockholder and depositor approvals secured and the subscription offering resolicitation complete, Columbia Financial's mutual-to-stock conversion and simultaneous Northfield Bancorp acquisition have cleared all major voting hurdles and are on track to close.

items.name: Gentherm Incorporated
items.headline: Gentherm enters $550M credit facility, references Modine SpinCo acquisition deal
whatChanged: Gentherm entered a Third Amended and Restated Credit Agreement (June 29, 2026) providing a $550M secured five-year revolving credit facility, replacing the prior Second Amended and Restated Credit Agreement dated June 10, 2022

The filing discloses a Proposed Transaction among Gentherm, Modine Manufacturing Company, and Modine's Performance Technologies business (SpinCo), with an S-4 registration statement filed July 2, 2026 including a proxy statement/prospectus for Gentherm shareholders

The credit agreement includes specific SpinCo-related provisions (Mirror SpinCo Matters, Mirror Indebtedness, Mirror Transactions), indicating the facility is structured to support the proposed acquisition
Pricing: Term SOFR/Alternative Currency Loans margin of 1.125%-2.000%, Base Rate margin of 0.125%-1.000%, commitment fee of 0.150%-0.250%, all based on consolidated net leverage ratio
The facility includes a $50M swing line sublimit and $30M letter of credit sublimit, with customary covenants including minimum interest coverage and maximum net leverage ratios
items.decisionTakeaway: Gentherm has secured a new $550M five-year revolver and publicly confirmed a proposed acquisition of Modine's Performance Technologies business (SpinCo), with an S-4/proxy filed the same day, signaling a transformative M&A move that will require shareholder approval and significantly expand the company's leverage capacity.

keyMetrics.termSOFRMargin: 1.125% to 2.000% per annum (based on consolidated net leverage ratio)
keyMetrics.baseRateMargin: 0.125% to 1.000% per annum (based on consolidated net leverage ratio)
keyMetrics.commitmentFee: 0.150% to 0.250% per annum (based on consolidated net leverage ratio)

items.name: Gentherm Incorporated
items.headline: Gentherm enters Third Amended Credit Agreement ahead of Modine/SpinCo deal
whatChanged: Gentherm entered a Third Amended and Restated Credit Agreement dated June 29, 2026, replacing the Second Amended and Restated Credit Agreement from June 10, 2022
Bank of America serves as Administrative Agent; JPMorgan, HSBC, PNC, and Wells Fargo are Co-Syndication Agents; BofA Securities is sole bookrunner
Agreement includes provisions for Mirror Loans, Mirror Indebtedness, and Mirror SpinCo Matters, tied to the pending Modine/SpinCo transaction
Pricing tiers set based on Consolidated Net Leverage Ratio, with initial Applicable Rate of 1.125% for Term SOFR loans and 0.150% commitment fee until Q2 2026 compliance certificate
Financial covenant includes Consolidated Net Leverage Ratio and Consolidated Interest Coverage Ratio; Available Amount permits investments, restricted payments, and debt prepayments
items.decisionTakeaway: The new credit agreement refinances and expands Gentherm's revolving facility to support the pending Modine/SpinCo acquisition, with terms and covenants structured around the combined company's leverage profile.

items.name: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc.
items.headline: Eos launches rights offering at $5.481/unit, conditional on JV agreement
whatChanged: Eos Energy commenced a transferable rights offering; each whole Right entitles the holder to purchase one Unit at a $5.481 subscription price, with each Unit consisting of one share of Common Stock and 0.4388 of a warrant (exercise price $5.481/share)

Rights expected to trade on Nasdaq under symbol 'EOSER' beginning July 6, 2026
Completion of the rights offering is conditional on execution of a JV Agreement and certain commercial framework guidelines by parties to the JV Transaction
Company retains the right to terminate the rights offering for any reason before expiration
items.decisionTakeaway: Eos is raising capital through a rights offering priced at $5.481/unit (share + fractional warrant), but completion is contingent on a JV agreement, adding deal-execution risk to the capital raise.

keyMetrics.unitComposition: 1 share Common Stock + 0.4388 warrant per Unit

items.name: Concentrix Corporation
items.headline: Q2 revenue up 2% but operating income falls 36% on restructuring
whatChanged: Revenue $2.46B (+1.9% YoY) with operating income declining to $95.4M from $148.3M (-35.7% YoY) due to $42.4M in severance costs affecting ~20,000 employees
H1 net income fell to $76.9M from $112.4M (-31.6% YoY) despite revenue growth of 3.6%, reflecting margin compression from restructuring and higher costs
Refinanced $600M of 6.650% Senior Notes due 2026 with 6.500% Senior Notes due 2029; voluntarily prepaid $100M of term loans and increased Securitization Facility from $700M to $750M
Non-core business classified as held for sale (~€15M divestiture expected H2 FY2026); recorded $6.9M loss on held for sale
Subsequent event: $0.36/share dividend declared June 29, 2026, payable August 4, 2026
items.decisionTakeaway: Concentrix is aggressively restructuring (~20,000 employees, $60M severance YTD) to address operating margin compression, with Q2 operating margin falling to 3.9% from 6.1%, while debt refinancing extends maturities but the cost-reduction benefits remain unrealized.

items.name: Gentherm Incorporated
items.headline: Gentherm S-4 filed for pending Merger; Barclays consents to fairness opinion inclusion
whatChanged: Gentherm filed an S-4 registration statement on July 2, 2026 relating to a Merger (as defined in the Registration Statement)
Barclays Capital Inc. consented to inclusion of its January 29, 2026 fairness opinion to Gentherm's Board as Annex C to the proxy statement/prospectus
The S-4/proxy covers the Merger transaction with references across Summary, Risk Factors, Background of Transactions, Financial Forecasts, and Opinion of Barclays sections
Barclays served as Gentherm's financial advisor for the transaction
items.decisionTakeaway: The S-4 registration statement advances Gentherm's pending merger toward shareholder vote, with Barclays' fairness opinion now formally consented for inclusion in the proxy materials.

items.name: Dollar Tree, Inc.

items.name: Arcturus Therapeutics Holdings Inc.
items.headline: Arcturus inks Thermo Fisher manufacturing deal for ARCT-032 CF program

Phase 3 program contingent on positive Phase 2 results; Arcturus expects to conduct Phase 3 through Thermo Fisher's PPD clinical research business
items.decisionTakeaway: The Thermo Fisher partnership de-risks ARCT-032's path from Phase 3 through potential commercial manufacturing, signaling program advancement for Arcturus' lead rare-disease asset without disclosed financial terms.

items.name: Alta Equipment Group Inc.

items.name: Lindsay Corporation
items.headline: Lindsay Q3 EPS $1.53 vs $1.78 YoY, revenue down 5%
whatChanged: Q3 revenue $160.8M, down 5% YoY; irrigation fell 7% (North America -11%, International -4%) while infrastructure grew 8% on road safety products
Operating income $18.5M (-22% YoY) with margin compressing to 11.5% from 14.0% on lower irrigation volume, input cost increases, and fixed-cost deleverage
Diluted EPS $1.53 vs $1.78 prior year; nine-month EPS $4.22 vs $5.79, with net earnings down 30% to $44.4M
Backlog rose to $136.1M from $117.1M prior year, driven by MENA irrigation project; ~$70M MENA project revenue still expected for FY2026
Share repurchases of $25.2M in Q3 bring YTD buybacks to $80.7M; management plans further cost-alignment actions
items.decisionTakeaway: Sequential improvement from Q2 (EPS $1.53 vs $1.15) signals troughing, but persistent North America irrigation weakness and margin compression keep the earnings trajectory negative YoY with no guidance change.

items.name: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc.

items.name: National Beverage Corp.
items.headline: FIZZ FY26: Revenue Down, Margins Hold, Special Dividend Declared
whatChanged: Net sales fell 1.7% YoY to $1,180.6M, driven by a 6.7% decline in case volume partially offset by a 5.2% increase in average selling price per case; Fiscal 2026 had 52 weeks vs 53 weeks in Fiscal 2025
Gross margin held flat at 37.0% despite higher packaging and ingredient costs, as price increases offset cost inflation
Operating income declined 2.3% YoY to $230.1M; diluted EPS fell 1.5% to $1.96 from $1.99
Board declared a new special cash dividend of $3.25 per share (payable July 30, 2026), matching the prior year's $304.1M special dividend
Cash position strengthened significantly to $349.5M from $193.8M, as no special dividend was paid during FY26 versus the $304.1M payout in FY25
items.decisionTakeaway: FY26 results show volume pressure offset by pricing and margin resilience, while the newly declared $3.25 special dividend signals continued shareholder returns despite flat earnings.

items.name: Hims & Hers Health, Inc.
items.headline: Hims & Hers enters receivables purchase facility with JPMorgan
whatChanged: Hims & Hers subsidiaries (XeCare LLC and Apostrophe Pharmacy LLC) entered into a Master Receivables Purchase Agreement with JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. dated July 1, 2026

The agreement establishes an uncommitted facility allowing HIMS to sell eligible receivables to JPMorgan on a non-recourse, true-sale basis, with JPMorgan assuming credit risk on account debtor non-payment
The facility is uncommitted — JPMorgan has sole discretion to accept or decline any purchase request — and may be terminated by either party on three business days' notice
Additional HIMS subsidiaries may join as sellers subject to JPMorgan consent and a Seller Joinder Agreement
The filing references a 'Credit Agreement' and a 'Program Limit' but does not disclose the facility's maximum size, pricing, or specific financial terms
items.decisionTakeaway: HIMS has secured a new uncommitted receivables monetization facility with JPMorgan that provides potential incremental liquidity by selling receivables on a true-sale basis, though the undisclosed program limit and JPMorgan's sole discretion make the immediate financial impact uncertain.

items.name: TIC Solutions, Inc.

items.name: LCI Industries
items.headline: Patrick Industries and LCI announce all-stock merger
whatChanged: Patrick Industries and LCI Industries announced a proposed all-stock merger; each LCI share converts to 1.2440 Patrick shares, with Patrick shareholders owning ~52% and LCI shareholders ~48% of the combined company
Combined company: ~$8.1B pro forma revenue, ~$1B adjusted EBITDA (12.6% margin), $508M free cash flow, 2.1x net leverage — figures reflect near-trough market conditions as of March 2026 LTM
$150M annual run-rate cost synergies identified (procurement/facilities ~half, G&A the balance); accretive in year one, fully realized by year three

Targeted closing in 1H 2027 subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals; Andy Nemeth (Patrick CEO) to lead combined company, Todd Cleveland as Chair, Johnny Sirpilla as Vice Chair; 12-member board split evenly

Aftermarket revenue targeted to grow from ~16% today to 20–25% of combined revenue over five years; management emphasized minimal product overlap and potential proactive divestitures in limited crossover categories
items.decisionTakeaway: A transformative all-stock merger creates an ~$8.1B revenue RV/marine/housing/transportation components leader with $150M in identified cost synergies and accretion in year one, though execution risk and regulatory approval remain key variables ahead of a 1H 2027 close.

items.name: Apogee Enterprises, Inc.
items.headline: Apogee Completes $115M Kalwall Acquisition
whatChanged: Apogee completed its previously announced acquisition of Kalwall Companies from the Keller family, with the transaction valued at up to $115 million.
Kalwall is a vertically integrated U.S. manufacturer of high-performance translucent daylighting solutions with a 71-year brand history and proprietary polymer and coating processes.
The acquisition adds daylighting capabilities and architectural community relationships to Apogee's architectural building products portfolio.
CEO and Executive Chair Don Nolan framed the deal as advancing Apogee's strategy and strengthening its position in attractive end markets.
items.decisionTakeaway: The closing of the up-to-$115M Kalwall acquisition (~13.6% of market cap) materially expands Apogee's architectural daylighting capabilities and should be assessed for incremental revenue, margin accretion, and integration risk in coming quarters.

items.name: Aon plc
items.headline: Aon adds $7.5B buyback; say-on-pay fails at AGM
whatChanged: Board approved a $7.5B increase to the share repurchase authorization, on top of ~$0.8B remaining as of March 31, 2026, bringing total available to ~$8.3B
Advisory say-on-pay resolution FAILED: 110.8M votes against vs 69.9M for, a notable governance signal
All 13 director nominees elected at the June 26, 2026 AGM; Ernst & Young ratified as auditor; share issuance and pre-emption opt-out authorities approved
CEO Greg Case's international assignment letter extended one year from June 30, 2026 to June 30, 2027
items.decisionTakeaway: Aon's $7.5B incremental buyback authorization (~9.8% of market cap) materially expands the capital return runway, while the failed advisory say-on-pay vote flags shareholder compensation concerns worth monitoring.

items.name: Nasdaq, Inc.
items.headline: Nasdaq amends and restates revolving credit agreement
whatChanged: Nasdaq entered into an Amended and Restated Credit Agreement dated June 30, 2026, replacing the Existing Credit Agreement originally dated December 16, 2022 (as amended four times through December 2024)
The new agreement establishes two separate revolving facilities — a Revolving A Facility and a Revolving B Facility — with multi-currency support including USD, EUR, GBP, NOK, SEK, DKK, and CAD
Pricing grid is debt-rating based with five levels; initially set at Pricing Level 3 (BBB+/Baa1): commitment fee 0.100%, benchmark loans 1.125%, LC fees 1.000%, ABR loans 0.125%
Includes sustainability-linked pricing adjustments tied to a KPI Metric (Percent SBT Spend) that can adjust the Applicable Rate annually
Leverage Ratio covenant defined as Total Indebtedness minus lesser of unrestricted cash or $600M, divided by Consolidated EBITDA
items.decisionTakeaway: Nasdaq refinanced its revolving credit facility into a new two-tranche structure with sustainability-linked pricing, maintaining liquidity flexibility without raising new debt.

keyMetrics.initialPricingLevel: Level 3 (BBB+/Baa1): commitment fee 0.100%, benchmark loans 1.125%, LC fees 1.000%, ABR loans 0.125%

Q3 2026 expected to include an additional $290M–$320M of IPR&D charges ($1.75–$1.95 EPS impact) from potential milestones and unclosed transactions, none of which was in prior FY2026 guidance

Prior FY2026 non-GAAP diluted EPS guidance of $14.25–$15.25 explicitly excluded future IPR&D beyond the ~$1.00 already embedded, so both the Q2 overrun (~$0.15) and the full Q3 charge are incremental headwinds to that range

items.decisionTakeaway: Biogen's Q2 IPR&D charge exceeds prior guidance and a new Q3 charge of $1.75–$1.95 per share will require a downward revision to FY2026 non-GAAP EPS guidance, though the spending reflects active business development and pipeline investment rather than core operational deterioration.

items.name: Transocean Ltd.
items.headline: Transocean secures $1B+ Equinor contract for three Cat D rigs
whatChanged: New agreement with Equinor for three harsh environment semisubmersible rigs on the Norwegian shelf, valued at over $1 billion in contract backlog over seven rig years
Base day rate of $399,000/day with adjustment provisions yielding an effective day rate exceeding $400,000/day at commencement

Transocean Enabler awarded 3-year program (Q1 2028 start); Transocean Encourage awarded 2-year program (Q1 2028 start); Transocean Endurance awarded 2-year program (Q2 2027 start after mobilization from Australia)
Agreement is conditional on license approvals; all three rigs are Cat D units originally purpose-built for Equinor
Prior comparable 8-K (June 16) announced only $185M in backlog across two rigs — this award is roughly 5x larger
items.decisionTakeaway: A $1B+ multi-rig Equinor award at >$400k/day effective day rates materially extends Transocean's harsh-environment backlog into 2028 and signals sustained pricing power in the Norwegian market.

items.name: Valaris Limited
items.headline: CFIUS Approval Received for Transocean-Valaris Merger
whatChanged: CFIUS approval received on June 29, 2026, satisfying a key closing condition for the Transocean-Valaris business combination
DOJ Second Request still pending; parties committed not to certify substantial compliance before July 31, 2026, with a 60-day waiting period after certification
Exchange ratio fixed at 15.235 Transocean shares per Valaris share; closing expected in second half of 2026
Shareholder approvals and remaining regulatory approvals still required
items.decisionTakeaway: CFIUS clearance removes a major regulatory hurdle for the Transocean-Valaris merger, but DOJ antitrust review via Second Request remains the key outstanding risk to timing and completion.

items.name: Bloomin' Brands, Inc.
items.headline: BLMN CHRO Jessica Mitory resigns effective Aug 17
whatChanged: Jessica Mitory notified the Company on June 26, 2026 of her resignation as SVP, Chief Human Resources Officer, effective August 17, 2026.
The filing states her departure is not due to any disagreement with the Company on operations, policies, or practices.
No successor was named in the filing; no other executive or financial changes were disclosed.
items.decisionTakeaway: The planned departure of Bloomin' Brands' CHRO is a non-disagreement officer transition with no disclosed operational or financial impact, leaving the broader turnaround strategy unchanged.

items.name: Ennis, Inc.
items.headline: Ennis Q1 FY26: Revenue up 1.4% on acquisitions, organic sales declined
whatChanged: Net sales rose 1.4% YoY to $98.6M, but organic volume fell $3.1M, offset by $4.5M+ incremental acquisition revenue (CFC, NEC/ESS)
Gross margin expanded 40 bps to 31.5%; operating income up 2.3% to $13.6M; diluted EPS $0.39 vs $0.38 prior year
No share repurchases in the quarter vs $5.0M repurchased in prior-year quarter; $7.2M remains authorized under buyback program
Operating cash flow surged to $21.2M from $8.0M YoY, driven by favorable working-capital changes; cash balance rose to $49.1M
B&D Litho lawsuit trial concluded March 2026 with judge dismissing tenant-maintenance claim; remaining breach-of-contract claim pending with $0.5M accrued
items.decisionTakeaway: Acquisition-driven top-line growth masked a $3.1M organic sales decline, while margin expansion and strong cash generation kept EPS flat-to-slightly-up, leaving the core print business still contracting.

keyMetrics.shareRepurchases: $0 in Q1 FY26 vs $5.0M in Q1 FY25
